campaigns.name: AI Adoption Roadmap
campaigns.objective: Position structured AI implementation as the antidote to pilot purgatory, driving demo requests from operations leaders seeking to scale beyond pilots
campaigns.targetAudience: VP/Director-level operations leaders at mid-market companies (100–1,000 employees, $10M–$100M revenue) who are resource-constrained on strategic tech decisions (<10 hours/week)
campaigns.positioning: Enterprise AI capabilities without enterprise complexity — a structured path from pilot to production that works with legacy systems, not against them
keyMessages: From pilot to production: A structured path to AI scaling that addresses the 20% gap
AI that works with your legacy systems, not against them
Unclear ROI within 30–90 days is a solvable problem, not a business constraint
contentThemes: The Real Cost of Manual Logistics Operations — data-driven ROI calculators showing fuel savings, stockout reduction, and efficiency gains
5 Signs Your AI Strategy Is Stuck in Pilot Purgatory — diagnostic content that positions structured roadmaps as the solution
How Mid-Size Logistics Companies Are Cutting Costs 25% with AI — case-study-style content with anonymized metrics and implementation timelines
phases: Phase 1: Awareness — establish presence and top-funnel capture with carousels and data-driven infographics on LinkedIn
Phase 2: Consideration — nurture and educate with blog posts, native document ads, and webinar registrations
Phase 3: Conversion — demo requests and SQL handoff triggered by pricing/demo visits, ROI calculator downloads, and webinar attendance
campaigns.currentPhase: Phase 1: Awareness — establish presence and top-funnel capture
successMetrics: LinkedIn impressions and saves on framework content
Demo request volume from top-funnel campaigns
Website-to-lead conversion rate improvement via form optimization

campaigns.name: Route Optimization Vertical
campaigns.objective: Own the route optimization category among mid-market operations leaders with a clear, measurable value proposition
campaigns.targetAudience: Operations leaders and technologists at mid-market distribution/logistics companies seeking 25%+ fuel savings within 90 days
campaigns.positioning: Stop guessing on routes. Start saving — practical route optimization that delivers 25% fuel savings in 90 days, proven in mid-market operations
keyMessages: Stop guessing on routes. Start saving — 25% fuel savings in 90 days
Route optimization that works with your existing fleet and drivers, not a complete fleet replacement
Integration is prerequisite for innovation — we integrate, not replace
contentThemes: Route Optimization: What Actually Works in 2026 — framework content comparing approaches and showing what drives real savings
The Hidden Costs of Route Inefficiency — data-driven content quantifying fuel, time, and wear-and-tear impacts
Implementation Timeline: What to Expect — transparency content that reduces purchase anxiety and builds trust
phases: Phase 1: Awareness — carousels and infographics on route optimization ROI, LinkedIn article deep-dives on methodology
Phase 2: Consideration — blog posts on best practices, native document ads with ROI calculators, webinar on implementation
Phase 3: Conversion — demo requests triggered by pricing page visits, ROI calculator downloads, and case study requests
campaigns.currentPhase: Phase 1: Awareness — establish presence and top-funnel capture
successMetrics: LinkedIn engagement (saves/shares) on route optimization content
Demo request conversion from pricing page visits

campaigns.name: Inventory Management Vertical
campaigns.objective: Position predictive inventory forecasting as the antidote to stockouts and dead stock, driving demo requests from mid-market retailers and distributors
campaigns.targetAudience: VP/Director-level operations and supply chain leaders at mid-market retailers and distributors struggling with stockouts and inventory carrying costs
campaigns.positioning: Inventory that moves. Not dead stock — predictive forecasting that reduces stockouts by 40% without over-investing in inventory
keyMessages: Inventory that moves. Not dead stock — 40% stockout reduction with predictive forecasting
Predictive forecasting that works with your legacy WMS, not a complete replacement
The data problems undermining mid-market AI projects — and how we solve them
contentThemes: Inventory Management: Best Practices for Mid-Size Warehouses — framework content on optimal stock levels and turnover
The Data Problems Undermining Mid-Market AI Projects — diagnostic content positioning data quality as the foundation of successful AI
Case Study: [Similar Company] Reduced Costs 30% in 6 Months — social proof with anonymized metrics and implementation timeline
phases: Phase 1: Awareness — data infographics on inventory carrying costs, LinkedIn articles on inventory optimization frameworks
Phase 2: Consideration — blog posts on best practices, native document ads with inventory health checklists, webinar on predictive forecasting
Phase 3: Conversion — demo requests triggered by case study downloads, inventory health checklist requests, and pricing page visits
campaigns.currentPhase: Phase 1: Awareness — establish presence and top-funnel capture
successMetrics: LinkedIn saves and shares on inventory cost content
Webinar registration and attendance for predictive forecasting deep dive

infrastructure.funnelStrategy: Top-funnel captures via 3-field forms (name, company, email) on high-value LinkedIn content. Mid-funnel nurtures via email sequences triggered by content downloads and website behavior. Bottom-funnel converts via 5–7 field demo forms, with progressive profiling to minimize friction while gathering qualification signals. All campaigns feed into a unified lead scoring and routing system.
conversionPaths: Primary: LinkedIn carousel/article → native document ad (gated asset) → email nurture sequence → 5–7 field demo request form → SQL handoff
Secondary: LinkedIn → blog post → newsletter signup → progressive profiling → webinar registration → demo request
infrastructure.nurtureApproach: Trigger-based email sequences (4–7 emails) focused on value delivery before conversion asks. Day 0, 3, 7, 14, 21, 30 cadence with specific content: frameworks, case studies, calculators. 3:1 rule — deliver value 3 times before asking for demo. Beyond first-name personalization: reference industry, previously downloaded content, and recent behavior.

scoringSignals: Company matches ICP (size + sector + zone): +10 points
Contact is decision-maker or influencer: +15 points
leadQualification.qualificationThreshold: Score 40–59 = MQL (nurture), 60–79 = SQL (24-hour outreach), 80+ = Hot lead (1-hour response)
leadQualification.routingGuidance: MQLs enter 14-day nurture sequence with educational content. SQLs trigger 3-day 4-touch demo request sequence. Hot leads receive executive outreach from SDR within 1 hour with personalized demo invite.

salesHandoff.owner: SDR team handles initial contact and scheduling. AEs own closed-won opportunities and account planning.
salesHandoff.process: CRM auto-creates lead record with score and signals. SDR reviews queue 24/5. Hot leads (80+) receive phone call within 1 hour. SQLs receive email + calendar link within 24 hours. MQLs enter nurture until score threshold crossed.

measurementPlan: Campaign-specific LinkedIn engagement metrics (impressions, saves, shares, profile visits)
Top-of-funnel to mid-funnel conversion rate (content downloads, webinar registrations)
Mid-funnel to bottom-funnel conversion rate (demo requests, qualified opportunities)
Lead scoring distribution and velocity (MQL→SQL conversion rate)
Form optimization impact (conversion rate by field count)
Overall cost per demo request and cost per qualified opportunity
